sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.92 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: (I.\,D.\,Macdonald). Every finite$p$-group has at least$p-1$conjugacy classes of maximum size. I.\,D.\,Macdonald (Proc. Edinburgh Math. Soc., 26 (1983), 233--239) constructed groups of order$2^n$for any$n 7$with just one conjugacy class of maximum size. Are there any examples with exactly$p-1$conjugacy classes of maximum size for odd$p$? { G.\,Fern\'andez--Alcober} Yes, there are, for$p=3$(A.\,Jai...
